checks.a_analysis: The output document contains the main title, date, legend headers, legend items, and source information found in the original document. No fabricated text or sections were identified.
The source document does not contain any phone numbers, email addresses, or URLs. The output document correctly reflects this absence and does not contain any fabricated contact information.
All sections of the single-page document are present in the output. The title, date, legend, source attribution, map image (with alt text), and logo (with alt text) are all included.

The legend data, which acts as a structured list of data rows, contains 10 items in the source document. The output JSON contains a list with exactly 10 corresponding items, capturing all data points from the legend.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the output matches the text in the source document exactly, with only minor acceptable whitespace changes (line breaks) in the sources section to improve readability.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the output JSON against the source image. All text blocks from the source, including the title, date, legend items, and sources, appear only once in the output. There is no evidence of duplicated paragraphs or list items.

The heading hierarchy in the output skips level 3. It proceeds from level 2 ('June 25, 2025') directly to level 4 ('Parcel Updates:'). Level 5 ('Most recent Quarter') follows. According to the instructions, a skip from H2 to H4 is a defect as it breaks the logical structure required for screen reader navigation.
checks.b_evidence_source: The document contains a title, a date, and a legend header ('Parcel Updates:').
checks.b_evidence_output: The output contains an H2 (element index 1) followed by an H4 (element index 2).

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any tables. The data in the legend is correctly formatted as a list.

The reading order in the output follows a logical path: Title -> Date -> Legend (Header, Subheader, List) -> Sources -> Map Image -> Logo Image. This linearizes the visual layout of the map and legend in a way that is coherent and flows logically.
The legend section containing the parcel update statistics is correctly identified and converted into an unordered list. All items and their respective counts match the source document accurately.
The source document does not contain any hyperlinks. No links were added to the output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML conversion linearizes the content of the source PDF. The title and date are placed at the top, followed by the legend (converted to a bulleted list) and the sources text. The primary graphic (the map) and logo are positioned at the end. While this is a significant departure from the PDF's fixed spatial layout where the legend and sources are overlaid on or placed next to the map, it represents a logical and standard transformation for accessible HTML and does not confuse the meaning of the document's information.
The source PDF and the converted HTML output do not contain any tables.

The primary visual content of the source document—the color-coded map of North Carolina and the NCGICC logo—is broken in the HTML output. The screenshot shows broken image icons with alt text placeholders ('A map of North Carolina showing...' and 'NCGICC logo') instead of the rendered graphics. This results in the loss of all spatial data and branding from the visual output.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains a central map of North Carolina counties and a NCGICC logo in the bottom right corner.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot displays two broken image icons with alt text 'A map of North Carolina showing the project status of parcel data by county, color-coded by the most recent quarter of the update.' and 'NCGICC logo'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Main body of the document, following the 'Sources' section.

checks.a_analysis: While the main title, date, and 'Parcel Updates' heading appear in both the 'Contents' navigation sidebar and the main body, this is considered intentional accessible design according to the evaluation criteria. No genuine body content, such as the legend list items or source text, is duplicated in the output.
